name: Vushka (Little Ear Dumplings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and salt in a bowl, stirring until well mixed.
2. Add the milk (or half a cup of whole milk), egg yolk, and oil to the flour mixture. Stir again until everything is combined.
3. Let the dough rest for 5 minutes.
4. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 10 minutes, or process in a food processor if preferred. Cover and let rest another 15 minutes.
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one-third of the dough into a rectangle that is 1/8 inch thick.
6. Turn the dough from the center outward to ensure even thickness throughout the rectangle.
7. Run your hand under the rolled dough to loosen it slightly, then dust with flour and flip over, dusting again.
8. With a sharp knife, cut the dough into 1½-inch squares.
9. Place a teaspoon of mushroom filling in each square, taking care not to touch the edges.
10. Fold diagonally to form a triangle, pinch together the two bottom corners, wrapping them around your thumb with the point up (forming small "ears").
11. Place on baking sheets covered with dish towels lightly dusted with flour. Repeat with remaining dough pieces; save scraps for last as they become tougher when worked.
12. Drop 10–12 vushka into 6 to 8 cups of rapidly boiling water and stir once with a wooden spoon (a metal spoon will lower the water temperature). Do not cover.
13. When the dumplings float, cook an additional minute, then remove with a slotted spoon to a wire rack; drain on a lightly oiled plate without crowding.
14. Repeat until all are cooked. Store covered and refrigerate. These can be frozen and reheated in boiling water (do not overcook when reheating).
